data.subject: Introduction — Ascendant Capital · New Multi-Asset Macro Strategy
data.body:
Thomas,

Nice to connect. I got your name from Suresh Iyer at Millbrook — he mentioned CPPIB is spending time on liquid real return and inflation-sensitive strategies for the pension book. I lead investor relations at Ascendant Capital and wanted to introduce our firm and a new fund we are quietly bringing to market.

Quick background: Ascendant is a New York-based multi-strategy manager, ~$28bn AUM across four active funds, founded in 2020 by Jonathan Reeves (formerly Millennium and Balyasny). We are institutional throughout — SS&C GlobeOp administration, KPMG audit, Goldman Sachs and JP Morgan prime brokerage.

The new fund is Fund V — a dedicated global multi-asset macro / real return vehicle. Target 10–12% net returns, equity beta below 0.20, capacity $4bn. The PM is Alexandra Voss (13 years at PIMCO running multi-asset real return, then two years running Ascendant's shadow book for this strategy).

I think this is squarely relevant to CPPIB's public commentary on wanting more liquid real-return exposure alongside the illiquid real assets book. Happy to send materials and set up a 45-minute intro call with Jonathan and Alex when it suits you.

Best,
Claire Whitfield
Head of Investor Relations · Ascendant Capital
c.whitfield@ascendantcapital.com · +1 (212) 555-0182
590 Madison Avenue, 28th Floor · New York, NY 10022

data.subject: Ascendant Fund V — Post-Call Follow-Up & Initial Questions
data.body:
Claire,

Thank you for the introductory call last month — Jonathan and Alexandra came across very well. The strategy thesis is compelling and the shadow portfolio numbers are encouraging, particularly given the macro backdrop over the last 12 months.

Following our internal discussion, we have a number of questions we would like addressed before we move to a formal due diligence process. I am copying Aisha Nkrumah who will be the analyst covering this review.

Strategy & Portfolio Construction

  1. Can you provide more detail on the regime identification framework — specifically, how allocation shifts between the four pillars are triggered and how quickly the portfolio can rebalance?
  2. The shadow portfolio shows a -0.3% drawdown in August 2024 — can you walk us through what drove it and how the position was managed?
  3. What is the expected correlation of Fund V to Fund II at the portfolio level? We want to understand whether there is meaningful overlap.

Operational & Structural

  4. Is the Fund V structure Cayman or Delaware? Can CPPIB invest via a separately managed account or co-investment structure?
  5. What are the governance arrangements — specifically, does the independent Risk Committee oversight that applies to the existing funds also apply to Fund V?

Track Record

  6. Can you share the full NDA for execution so we can access the GIPS-compliant PIMCO track record?

We would aim to revert with a fuller set of DD questions once we have reviewed your responses.

Thanks,
Thomas Brennan
Director, Hedge Fund Investments · CPP Investments
t.brennan@cppib.com · +1 (416) 868-4212
One Queen Street East, Suite 2500 · Toronto, ON M5C 2W5

data.subject: Ascendant Fund V — Investment Committee Clearance & Meeting Request
data.body:
Claire,

I am pleased to share that following our internal review process, CPPIB's External Portfolio Management committee has approved Ascendant Capital Fund V for advancement to formal investment committee consideration.

This is a significant step. To progress, we would like to schedule a formal in-person meeting in New York with the Ascendant senior team. Robert Yuen, our Head of Alternative Investments, will attend alongside Thomas, Priya, and James Leclerc.

The agenda we have in mind:

  1. Fund V strategy deep-dive — Alex Voss to lead (45 mins)
  2. Risk framework and shadow portfolio attribution — CRO Dr. Vasquez to present (30 mins)
  3. Firm overview, team stability, and operational infrastructure — Jonathan Reeves and COO Torres (30 mins)
  4. Portfolio fit discussion — how Fund V complements CPPIB's existing alternatives book (30 mins)
  5. Open Q&A (15 mins)

Outstanding items we will need resolved before or at the meeting:

  - Final GIPS-compliant performance document (including Q1 2026 shadow returns)
  - Confirmation of CFTC exempt CPO status for Fund V (please attach the Skadden filing letter)
  - SMA term sheet (Rachel Kwan and Sidley Austin are in discussion; please accelerate)
  - Brazil DI stress scenario analysis and EM rates liquidity waterfall (James Leclerc)

Our IC calendar has moved. The original April/May window has been re-planned to Wednesday, August 12, 2026 to give committee members appropriate time with the refreshed materials. We would like the Ascendant meeting to sit approximately two weeks before IC — Thomas will follow up with proposed dates around mid/late July 2026.

We look forward to progressing this.

Best regards,
Margaret Osei
Managing Director, External Portfolio Management · CPP Investments

data.subject: Ascendant Fund V — Brazil DI Volatility Note Ahead of Executive Meeting
data.body:
Claire,

Quick note following our alignment call on Wednesday. Two things I want to flag ahead of the executive meeting so Alex and Elena are not caught cold.

Brazil DI — June / early July 2026 volatility. Leclerc has been watching the recent widening in DI spreads and the intraday moves in the past three weeks. He is broadly comfortable with how Fund V is positioned — this is not a change of mind — but he is going to raise it in the room and he wants a specific view on: (i) how the current market resembles or differs from the stress scenario Alex walked through in December, (ii) whether the 10-day liquidation assumption still holds in the June/July tape, and (iii) what the recovery path looks like from here. He does not need a long answer, but he needs a specific one.

IC narrative. As I mentioned on the call, a couple of our IC members are less familiar with liquid macro strategies. Robert asked me to reinforce with you that the opening framing of the executive meeting presentation is the single most important thing — not the strategy label, but the pension portfolio problem Fund V is solving. Jonathan handled this brilliantly in the October presentation; I would like the executive meeting opening to hit the same notes.

Everything else is on track. GIPS through Q2 is with us, the SMA executed copy is with our legal team, and I will chase Priya for the compliance ack on the sanctions screening once your team sends it.

See you at the executive meeting.

Best,
Thomas Brennan
Director, Hedge Fund Investments · CPP Investments
